description: Violin plot of the 2025 SPG score distribution for each of four locales (City, Suburb, Town, Rural), one density curve per row on a shared 20–100 score axis; curve width shows relative frequency, not a count. City is highlighted in navy as the widest-spread locale; the others are neutral grey.

title.text: City schools post the widest spread of SPG scores
title.subtitle: Distribution of School Performance Grade scores by locale, all NC schools, 2025. Curve width is relative frequency, not a count.

layer.comment: Locale label as a left-gutter text layer. VL 5.21 ignores labelAlign on left-oriented row-facet HEADERS (they right-align), so — as on the sparklines spec — the header is dropped and a fixed-pixel text mark at x:0 puts every locale name on the same left edge as the title. The x-domain opens to 8 so the violin sits to the right of the label in its own clear zone. Aggregate-to-one-row avoids overplotting the label across every density sample.

usermeta.source: Source: NCDPI public accountability data (School Performance Grades), 2025. Style-guide demo.
